highest_risk_mismatches.summary: pyRing truncates after moving each detector into its own arrival-time frame, while the local SBI pipeline still builds a shared fixed-length detector representation.
Public pyRing GW150914 runs use 4096 Hz and 0.2 s, while the local pipeline uses 2048 Hz and 0.1 s.
Public pyRing GW150914 runs use 100-500 Hz bandpassing and ACF estimation from data chunks; the local pipeline does not yet mirror that full preprocessing path.

medium_risk_mismatches.summary: pyRing uses reference-amplitude scaling, while the local injection config specifies A_220 directly in physical units.
Public pyRing runs use acf-simple-norm=1, which the local PSD-derived covariance path does not explicitly mirror.

recommended_next_step.title: replicate pyRing detector-local truncation semantics in the SBI observation builder
recommended_next_step.reason: This is the most plausible way to preserve the recovered SNR while restoring round-1 TSNPE contraction.
